sections.subtitle: Team Form Analysis
sections.text: Oud-Heverlee Leuven have struggled recently, with only 2 wins in their last 5 matches and sitting at 15th in the league with 7 points. RSC Anderlecht, in contrast, are in better form with 4 wins and 2 draws in their last 8 matches, positioned 2nd with 14 points.

sections.subtitle: Head-to-Head Record
sections.text: In the last 2 head-to-head matches, both ended in draws (0-0 and 1-1), indicating closely contested games. Anderlecht have a slight historical edge with no losses in the last 5 matchups against Leuven.

sections.subtitle: Scoring and Defensive Stats
sections.text: Leuven have scored only 7 goals this season (14th in the league) and conceded 16 (15th), showing defensive vulnerabilities. Anderlecht have a stronger attack with 13 goals and a better defense, conceding just 8 goals, suggesting they are likely to dominate.

sections.subtitle: Match Statistics Trends
sections.text: Recent matches for both teams often see fewer than 2.5 goals, especially in head-to-heads (4 of 5 under 2.5). Both teams also average high yellow cards per game (Leuven 3.125, Anderlecht similar), and corners are generally under 10.5 in their matches.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Half-time overview
halftimeSections.text: At half-time, the score remains goalless between Oud-Heverlee Leuven and RSC Anderlecht at The King Power AT Den Dreef Stadium. Despite the lack of goals, the first half showcased contrasting styles of play. Leuven, the home side, created more dangerous opportunities, including a significant chance that they failed to convert. Anderlecht, on the other hand, dominated possession and made frequent entries into the final third but struggled to translate their control into clear-cut chances. The game remains finely balanced as both teams head into the break.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Home team performance: Oud-Heverlee Leuven
halftimeSections.text: Oud-Heverlee Leuven displayed a more direct and threatening approach in the first half. They managed to create a big chance and registered several shots on target, showing their intent to break the deadlock. Their defensive structure held firm despite less possession, with notable contributions in clearances and interceptions. Players like Óscar Gil stood out with their attacking involvement, though a missed opportunity could haunt them. Leuven's ability to capitalize on limited possession will be key in the second half.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Away team performance: RSC Anderlecht
halftimeSections.text: RSC Anderlecht controlled the game for large periods, dominating the ball and making numerous entries into the attacking third. However, their inability to convert possession into meaningful chances was evident, as they failed to register a single shot on target. Defensively, they looked solid, winning a high percentage of duels, but disciplinary issues with a couple of yellow cards could pose a risk. Anderlecht will need to sharpen their final product if they are to unlock Leuven's defense in the second half.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Attack momentum analysis
halftimeSections.text: The first half saw fluctuating momentum between the two sides. Early on, Leuven showed intent with some promising attacking spells, pushing forward with purpose. As the game progressed, Anderlecht took control with sustained periods of pressure, particularly in the middle of the half, where they camped in the opposition half. However, Leuven responded with dangerous bursts, especially in the latter stages of the half, regaining momentum at key moments. Towards the end, Anderlecht again pushed forward with intensity, but neither side could find the decisive breakthrough.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Key areas to watch
halftimeSections.text: The second half could hinge on Leuven's ability to convert their limited but high-quality chances, especially after missing a big opportunity earlier. Anderlecht must find a way to turn their dominance in possession and territorial advantage into genuine goal-scoring threats. Discipline will also be critical for the away side, as further bookings could disrupt their rhythm. Substitutions and tactical adjustments may play a significant role, with both managers likely to tweak their setups to exploit the other's weaknesses.
